items.title: As war between Israel and Hezbollah escalates, life in Lebanon poised to get 'uglier day by day'
items.summary_auto: Almost two weeks into the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran, the conflict in Lebanon is quickly escalating and expanding into more regions of the country. It began after the Iranian-backed militant group Hezbollah fired rockets into Israel, pulling Lebanon back into a major conflict with Israel.

items.title: Mexican trade mission in May to visit Montreal, Toronto, Vancouver
items.summary_auto: A Mexican trade delegation is scheduled to launch a three-city tour in Canada at the beginning of May that will also include bilateral trade talks between government officials, Mexico's Secretary of Economy announced Thursday.

items.title: Telus probes cybersecurity incident that 'ShinyHunters' group claims responsibility for
items.summary_auto: Canadian telecommunications and business services firm Telus is investigating a cybersecurity incident involving unauthorized access to some ‌of its systems, a company spokesperson said on Thursday.

items.title: Carney announces $35B for defence, infrastructure in Canada's North
Winnipeg to host Juno awards in 2027
items.summary_auto: Many of Canada's brightest music stars will descend on Winnipeg for the Juno Awards in 2027.

items.title: How astronaut Jeremy Hansen's moon mission is inspiring students at his Ingersoll, Ont., alma mater
items.summary_auto: A lunchtime Lego project at Jeremy Hansen's former high school in Ingersoll, Ont., has grown into a mission to follow his upcoming Artemis II moon journey, and the Canadian astronaut's parents paid ode to the students' efforts by visiting their son's alma mater — with a special package.

items.title: KPop Demon Hunters sequel on the way, Netflix confirms
items.summary_auto: Netflix confirmed on Thursday that a sequel to KPop Demon Hunters, the streaming platform's most watched film of all time, is officially in development.

items.title: Silence from Ottawa 'shameful' after report of Canadian camp hit by missile: Conservative MP
items.summary_auto: Conservative defence critic James Bezan says it's "really shameful" the federal government did not tell the public that the airbase in Kuwait, where the Canadian Armed Forces have a camp, was hit by an Iranian missile attack on March 1.

items.title: B.C. chiefs tell MP Aaron Gunn to 'chillax' about land acknowledgments
items.summary_auto: The chiefs of four British Columbia First Nations have told Conservative member of Parliament Aaron Gunn to "chillax" after he criticized land acknowledgments spoken before public events.

items.title: Ryan Wedding co-accused linked to Toronto police corruption probe moved to special jail unit
items.summary_auto: An alleged drug smuggler for accused kingpin Ryan Wedding – recently linked to a man charged in a sprawling Toronto police corruption probe – has been moved to a special jail unit. Gurpreet Singh's lawyer told a court about the development, days after CBC News revealed Singh's ties to Project South.

items.title: Oscars 2026: Who might win, who should win — and what to know ahead of your Oscars party
Sinners or OBAA? Predicting the most unpredictable Oscars in years
items.summary_auto: The results of the 2026 Academy Awards feel harder to guess than any ceremony in living memory. To help you prepare for what is sure to be a nail-biting, shocker of a night, we've broken down the biggest categories — and the razor's-edge battles taking place in them.

items.title: This Yellowknife athlete just set 2 records by hopping 66 metres on his knuckles at the Arctic Winter Games
items.summary_auto: An athlete from the Northwest Territories has set two new records by hopping 66.142 metres on his knuckles at the Arctic Winter Games.

items.title: Israeli military drops charges against soldiers accused of sexual, physical abuse of Palestinian detainee
items.summary_auto: Israel's military on Thursday said it was dropping charges against five soldiers accused of sexually assaulting a Palestinian detainee in an incident partially caught on camera. The decision closed a case that has bitterly divided the country since the soldiers were arrested in 2024 at the notorious Sde Teiman military prison.

items.title: Part of Whistler ski resort closed following Thursday morning rockslide
items.summary_auto: Whistler Blackcomb owner Vail Resorts says the rockslide happened before the ski resort opened and that no one was injured.

items.title: Edmonton police chief faces backlash for 'educational' meeting with Israeli police
items.summary_auto: Edmonton’s police chief is facing criticism for travelling to Israel in February to meet with state policing officials as part of a counterterrorism delegation.

items.title: Former rapper's political party sweeps Nepal election
items.summary_auto: A political party created just four years ago and led by an ex-rapper has swept Nepal's parliamentary poll, results published by the electoral commission on Thursday showed.

items.title: FBI investigating Detroit-area synagogue attack as 'targeted act of violence against the Jewish community'
items.summary_auto: The FBI says it is investigating Thursday's attack at a Detroit-area synagogue as a "targeted act of violence against the Jewish community," but have not yet been able to identify the attacker or confirm a motive.

items.title: Rice shipment heading for Toronto food banks struck near Iran, CEO says
items.summary_auto: Effects of the Mideast conflict are now reverberating in Toronto after a shipment of rice meant for the city's Daily Bread Food Bank was struck Wednesday in the Strait of Hormuz near Iran, the food bank's CEO says.

items.title: New lawful access bill would give police, CSIS more powers to track suspects online
items.summary_auto: The Liberal government has introduced a new lawful access bill that it says will help police and security services track and identify people who may be using tools like social media or artificial intelligence to commit crimes or threaten national security.

items.title: Canada's wheelchair curling team moves to 8-0, clinches top playoff seed at Milano-Cortina Paralympics
items.summary_auto: Canada's wheelchair curling team made Paralympics history on Thursday as the first team to complete round-robin play without a loss, downing the United States 7-3 for a 9-0 record in Milan Cortina.

items.title: Canada's wheelchair curlers make Paralympic history with unbeaten round-robin
items.summary_auto: Canada's wheelchair curling team made Paralympics history on Thursday as the first team to complete round-robin play without a loss, downing the United States 7-3 for a 9-0 record in Milan Cortina.

items.title: CRTC to eliminate fees when cancelling or switching cellphone and internet plans
items.summary_auto: Canada's telecommunications regulator says it will prevent companies from charging customers when they cancel, change or activate plans.

items.title: NASA targeting no earlier than April 1 to send astronauts around the moon in Artemis II mission
items.summary_auto: After several delays to the mission that will take four astronauts around the moon, NASA held a press conference today announcing that Artemis II is on track to launch as early as April 1.

items.title: St. John's hockey league doles out 'unprecedented' suspensions for Caps-Breakers rumble
items.summary_auto: The St. John's Junior Hockey League has dropped the hammer on the Southern Shore Breakers following a fight-filled playoff game on Saturday night that saw referees issue nearly 600 minutes in penalties.

items.title: N.S. developer ordered to remove 2 extra storeys he built without permission
items.summary_auto: Halifax council has decided a developer must rip out a Wyse Road building's top two storeys, which it did not have permission to build. A report from municipal staff said the move has never been done on this scale in the city.

items.title: After 5 years of no clean drinking water, Oneida pipeline project approved
items.summary_auto: Construction to extend the Lake Huron Primary Water Supply System pipeline to the Oneida Nation of the Thames will soon be underway after a London, Ont., company won a bid for the federal contract last week. Officials aim for the water to start flowing to the First Nation by next year.

items.title: Hockey player who lost family in Rhode Island shooting scores winning goal in OT
items.summary_auto: High school senior hockey player Colin Dorgan, wearing a patch on his jersey honouring the three family members he lost in a Rhode Island ice rink shooting last month, scored a game-winning goal in double overtime Wednesday that advanced the Blackstone Valley Co-op team out of the semifinals in Providence, R.I.

items.title: Niagara Regional chair resigns over ownership of signed copy of Hitler's Mein Kampf
items.summary_auto: Niagara Regional Chair Bob Gale has abruptly resigned, just hours after anti-racism groups in Niagara demanded he apologize for owning a signed copy of Adolf Hitler's infamous manifesto Mein Kampf, which outlined the Nazi leader's racist and anti-Semitic world views before he oversaw mass murder including the killing of six million Jewish people during the Second World War.

items.title: PWHL to reach national U.S. TV audience with Scripps Sports to broadcast neutral site game in Detroit
items.summary_auto:
The PWHL announced that its neutral-site game at Detroit on March 28 between the New York Sirens and Montreal Victoire will
be the first accessible to a national U.S. television audience.

items.title: Ethiopia landslides leave at least 50 dead, dozens missing
items.summary_auto: At least 50 people have died and 125 others are missing after landslides hit three districts in southern Ethiopia following a week of heavy rains, a local official said Thursday.

items.title: Oil prices jump to $100 US a barrel, stocks fall on Thursday
items.summary_auto: With no clear end in sight, the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran is sending oil prices back to $100 US per barrel, as stocks sunk worldwide on Thursday.

items.title: What’s old is new again: Winless Canadian junior hockey team makes ownership change
items.summary_auto: The GOHL's Sarnia Legionnaires finished 0-50 for the 2025-2026 GOHL season, and immediately made a change at the top of the organization.

items.title: Russia sentences 19 people for deadly concert hall attack that killed 149 people
items.summary_auto: A court in Moscow on Thursday convicted 19 people of involvement in the 2024 shooting rampage at a Moscow concert hall that killed 149 people and wounded over 600 in one of the deadliest attacks in the capital in years.

items.title: Translating, restraining kids, teaching multiple grades at once: Alberta teachers describe complex classrooms
items.summary_auto: Data rarely tells the full story. So when CBC News emailed a questionnaire to tens of thousands of Alberta teachers this January, we invited them to share stories to illustrate what classroom complexity actually looks like for them. More than 4,000 teachers participated.

items.title: Manitoba Health Minister says banning paid plasma an option after 2 deaths
items.summary_auto: Paid plasma could be banned in Manitoba, the province's health minister said after two fatal adverse reactions at Winnipeg collection centres were reported to Health Canada.

items.title: Can Trump's 'gunboat diplomacy' stop Iran from blocking the flow of oil? Not likely, say experts
'It guts us’: Curator fears for future of Joggins Fossil Institute after provincial funding cuts
items.summary_auto: The curator of the Joggins Fossil Institute says a 20 per cent cut to its provincial funding will devastate operations and force the cancellation of educational programs that reach hundreds of students each year.

items.title: 'That never happened': Rapper K'naan testifies in his own defence at sexual assault trial in Quebec City
items.summary_auto: Rapper K'naan, whose real name is Keinan Abdi Warsame, is accused of sexually assaulting a woman in a Quebec City hotel room in 2010. Testifying in his own defence, he said the incident never happened.

items.title: Putin is trying to pitch himself as peacemaker in Iran, but Russia also sees opportunity
items.summary_auto: The Kremlin wants to exert its influence in the Middle East and has publicly said its support for Iran, a strategic ally, is unwavering. But experts say there are limits to how far Moscow will go: It does not want to antagonize Washington, and it also stands to gain economically from a global energy crisis.

items.title: How shipping companies are navigating danger in the Strait of Hormuz
items.summary_auto: As the Mideast conflict escalates near the Strait of Hormuz, merchant ships and their workers have been caught in the crosshairs.

items.title: What's climate change doing to avalanches and how we predict them?
items.summary_auto: A series of tragic deaths in Canada, the U.S. and Europe is showing how tricky avalanches still are to predict. Will that change with global warming?

items.title: How remote First Nations are working with Ornge to improve medical transportation
items.summary_auto: A new program in remote First Nations across northwestern Ontario aims to improve access to medical transportation and emergency medical service training. Communities are leading the emergency first response team (EFRT) program in partnership with the air ambulance service Ornge. Here's how the EFRT aims to improve health-care access in fly-in First Nations.

items.title: The Oscars are giving an award for best casting. About time, casting directors say
items.summary_auto: For the first time since 2001, the Academy Awards will be giving away a trophy for a brand new category. The professionals responsible for assembling a well-fitting cast say they're glad to have an award that puts their craft in the spotlight.
